exercises.title: Hello World
Variables & Data Types
exercises.description: Declare int x = 5, float pi = 3.14, char letter = 'A'. Print all three on separate lines.
exercises.hint: Use %d for int, %f for float, %c for char with printf.

exercises.title: If-Else Conditionals
exercises.description: Given int score = 85, print Grade: B if score >= 80, otherwise print Needs Improvement.

exercises.title: For Loop
exercises.description: Print numbers from 0 to 4 using a for loop, each prefixed with Iteration.
exercises.hint: Use for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) { printf("Iteration %d\n", i); }

exercises.title: Functions
exercises.description: Create a void greet() function that prints Hello from a custom function!. Call it from main.
exercises.hint: Define void greet() { printf("Hello from a custom function!\n"); } above main.

exercises.title: Arrays
exercises.description: Declare int numbers[5] = {10, 20, 30, 40, 50}. Print the first and second elements.

exercises.title: Strings
exercises.description: Declare char msg[] = "Hello" and print it using %s format specifier.

exercises.title: Pointers
exercises.description: Declare int x = 10 and a pointer int *p = &x. Print the value of x via the pointer using dereferencing.
exercises.hint: Use printf("%d\n", *p); to dereference the pointer.

exercises.title: Structures
exercises.description: Create a struct Person with char name[30] and int age. Create a Person with name Sanjith and age 25, then print them.

exercises.title: Enum Types
exercises.description: Create an enum Week {Mon, Tue, Wed}. Set today = Tue and print its integer value.
exercises.hint: enum Week {Mon, Tue, Wed}; enum Week today = Tue; printf("%d\n", today);
